questions.q: Aruna Asaf Ali is best remembered for hoisting the national flag during which movement?
questions.explanation: She hoisted the national flag at Gowalia Tank during the Quit India Movement of 1942.

questions.q: In which year did Aruna Asaf Ali hoist the flag at Gowalia Tank in Bombay?
questions.explanation: She hoisted the flag at Gowalia Tank in 1942 at the launch of the Quit India Movement.

questions.q: What did Aruna Asaf Ali do after the British arrested the Congress leadership in 1942?
options: She went underground to direct the agitation
questions.explanation: She went underground and helped direct the Quit India agitation.

questions.q: By what honorific is Aruna Asaf Ali often remembered?
options: The Grand Old Lady of the freedom movement
questions.explanation: She is remembered as the Grand Old Lady of the freedom movement.

questions.q: In which city did Aruna Asaf Ali raise the flag at Gowalia Tank?
questions.explanation: She raised the flag at Gowalia Tank Maidan in Bombay.

questions.q: Aruna Asaf Ali was a leader of which movement of 1942?
questions.explanation: She was a prominent leader of the Quit India Movement.

questions.q: Whom did Aruna Asaf Ali marry, giving her the surname Asaf Ali?
questions.explanation: She married Asaf Ali, a Congress leader and lawyer, taking his surname.

questions.q: What act made Aruna Asaf Ali an iconic figure of 1942?
options: Hoisting the flag at Gowalia Tank
questions.explanation: Her hoisting of the flag at Gowalia Tank became an iconic image of the Quit India Movement.

questions.q: During the freedom struggle, Aruna Asaf Ali was known for which quality?
options: Her loyalty to the British Crown
questions.explanation: She was admired for her courage and defiance, especially during the Quit India Movement.

questions.q: What broad cause did Aruna Asaf Ali devote herself to?
questions.explanation: She devoted herself to India's freedom struggle against British rule.

questions.q: On what date did Aruna Asaf Ali hoist the flag at Gowalia Tank?
questions.explanation: She hoisted the flag on 9 August 1942, the day after the Quit India resolution was passed.

questions.q: Which underground nationalist journal did Aruna Asaf Ali help edit during the 1940s?
questions.explanation: She was associated with editing the underground monthly Inquilab during the freedom struggle.

questions.q: What was Aruna Asaf Ali's maiden name before her marriage?
questions.explanation: She was born Aruna Ganguly into a Bengali Brahmo family.

questions.q: In which earlier movement was Aruna Asaf Ali first arrested in 1930?
options: The Salt Satyagraha / Civil Disobedience Movement
questions.explanation: She was arrested in 1930 for her part in the Salt Satyagraha during the Civil Disobedience Movement.

questions.q: Aruna Asaf Ali's husband Asaf Ali later served independent India as what?
questions.explanation: Asaf Ali became independent India's first ambassador to the United States.

questions.q: After independence, which high civic office did Aruna Asaf Ali hold in 1958?
questions.explanation: She became the first Mayor of Delhi in 1958.

questions.q: During the underground phase of Quit India, what risk did Aruna Asaf Ali face?
options: Arrest and confiscation of her property by the British
questions.explanation: A warrant was issued for her arrest and her property was attached while she remained underground.

questions.q: To which political tendency did Aruna Asaf Ali lean in the years after independence?
questions.explanation: She gravitated toward socialist and leftist politics for a period after independence.

questions.q: Which religious-reform community was Aruna Asaf Ali's family associated with?
questions.explanation: She came from a Bengali Brahmo Samaj family background.

questions.q: Aruna Asaf Ali's flag-hoisting symbolized what about the Quit India Movement?
options: That it would continue even with leaders jailed
questions.explanation: Her defiant act signalled that the movement would carry on despite the arrest of its leaders.

questions.q: Which other women contemporaries shared Aruna Asaf Ali's prominence in the Quit India era?
options: Rani Lakshmibai and Begum Hazrat Mahal
questions.explanation: Usha Mehta of the secret Congress Radio and Sucheta Kripalani were fellow Quit India activists.

questions.q: In which decade was Aruna Asaf Ali most active in the underground freedom struggle?
questions.explanation: Her most celebrated underground activity came in the 1940s during and after Quit India.

questions.q: In what year was Aruna Asaf Ali born?
questions.explanation: Aruna Asaf Ali was born in 1909 at Kalka in Punjab.

questions.q: India's highest civilian honour, awarded to Aruna Asaf Ali in 1997, was which?
questions.explanation: She was awarded the Bharat Ratna in 1997, the year after her death, the honour given posthumously.

questions.q: In what year did Aruna Asaf Ali die?
questions.explanation: Aruna Asaf Ali died in 1996.

questions.q: Aruna Asaf Ali received the Lenin Peace Prize in which earlier decade, reflecting her leftist ties?
questions.explanation: She was awarded the international Lenin Peace Prize in 1964.

questions.q: The marriage of Aruna Ganguly to Asaf Ali was notable in its time because it was what?
options: A marriage within the same village
A marriage arranged by the British
An inter-faith marriage crossing community lines
questions.explanation: Their union was an inter-faith marriage between a Hindu Brahmo woman and a Muslim man, defying social convention.

questions.q: After 1942, Aruna Asaf Ali eventually surfaced from underground only after what kind of order against her was withdrawn?
options: The warrant for her arrest was withdrawn
questions.explanation: She emerged from hiding after the warrant for her arrest was finally withdrawn in 1946.

questions.q: Which prestigious international award named after a UN figure did Aruna Asaf Ali also receive for promoting peace?
options: The Jawaharlal Nehru Award for International Understanding
questions.explanation: She received the Jawaharlal Nehru Award for International Understanding in 1991.

questions.q: What is the enduring significance of Aruna Asaf Ali in the freedom struggle?
options: She symbolizes women's bold leadership in the final phase of the struggle
She founded the Indian National Army
questions.explanation: She stands as a symbol of women's courageous leadership during the climactic Quit India phase.
